global_primitives.name: Trigger source and case matching
global_primitives.purpose: Recognise the operative event—not merely the channel—and match it using case name, short name, case number, parties, sender, document title, court, judge, or department.

global_primitives.name: Incoming, outgoing, and hard-copy trigger rule
global_primitives.purpose: Treat received communications, firm-created work product, and scanned/uploaded material as possible triggers; route the content to a more specific trigger when one exists.

global_primitives.name: One trigger, many atomic actions
global_primitives.purpose: Create separate records for each deadline, reminder, document, communication, filing/service step, status update, review, vendor step, and completion event.

global_primitives.name: Date and service-method engine
global_primitives.purpose: Separate base period, operative date, day type, direction, service adjustment, weekend/holiday handling, and court/order overrides. Never rely on a blind shorthand when the underlying rule matters.

global_primitives.name: Extension and changed-date control
global_primitives.purpose: Preserve the original date as history, confirm the new agreement in writing, recalculate dependent dates, and create replacement entries without erasing the audit trail.

global_primitives.name: Filing and service completion
global_primitives.purpose: A draft or upload is not completion; require acceptance, proof of service, transmission evidence, receipt, signed document, or other defined evidence.

global_primitives.name: Document sample selection
global_primitives.purpose: Retrieve the current official form when applicable and the best approved Jack Law sample by document type, posture, forum, claim type, judge, and factual similarity; then populate verified case facts only.

global_primitives.name: Communication memorialisation
global_primitives.purpose: Capture audience, objective, channel, timing, tone, exact ask, concessions, what not to say, oral memorialisation, follow-up, and completion evidence.

global_primitives.name: Human review and automation boundary
global_primitives.purpose: State what CaseSync may do automatically, what it may propose, who verifies extraction/calculation, who reviews substance, who approves, and when uncertainty escalates.

global_primitives.name: Expected-event and stale-item control
global_primitives.purpose: Define what event should arrive next, when to follow up if it does not, how overdue items surface, and what proves the loop is closed.

global_primitives.name: Multi-party, multi-set, and forum separation
global_primitives.purpose: Keep distinct parties, discovery instruments, sets, courts, judges, and provider rules separate unless Jack expressly adopts a shared action.

global_primitives.name: Case-development radar
global_primitives.purpose: At each stage, notice evidence, witnesses, coworkers, records, damages, experts, themes, pressure, settlement value, and trial-readiness opportunities; tag rather than derail Round 1.

global_primitives.name: Client-management and client-development radar
global_primitives.purpose: Notice client cooperation, status, education, reminders, referrals, relatives, former coworkers, community, testimonials/UGC, advocacy, charity, work, or deeper collaboration; keep development as a separate consent-aware round.

global_primitives.name: Source hierarchy and contradiction control
global_primitives.purpose: Jack's current correction controls firm practice. Preserve prior direct statements, approved samples, staff workflow, and provisional inferences with status and resolve only consequential conflicts.

global_primitives.name: Oversight and audit
global_primitives.purpose: Make the work visible beyond simple upload: link source, task, calendar, draft, reviewer, completion proof, version history, and periodic case-wide omission review.

• RFAs: proposed 30 days plus authorized service extension; verify current CCP 2033.250.

• Create a separate package for each instrument and set.
• Two-week tickler 14 calendar days before due date.
• One-week tickler 7 calendar days before due date.
• Complete client discovery call within first 7 calendar days.
• Open response shells, questionnaire, document list, verifications, and workflow same day.

BRANCHES / EXCEPTIONS
• Which separate instruments/sets?
• Valid proof of service?
• Personal/electronic/mail/overnight?
• Shortened time/order?
• Subpoena/deposition branch?

project.title: JackLaw Streamlined Trigger → Rule / Procedure → Action Master List
project.purpose: Capture Jack’s chronological litigation knowledge with the fewest fields necessary before a separate developer-recognition mapping phase.
project.recognition_layer: Deferred. Search terms, aliases, OCR cues, extraction fields, and channel-specific detection are preserved as background and will be finalized with the developer.
global_standards: Use a short chronological trigger name in ordinary litigation parlance.
The trigger is the operative event, not merely the email, scan, upload, call, or outgoing document carrying it.
State the hard legal/court rule and the JackLaw procedure together in one field, clearly labeled when both exist.
Create one separately typed action for each calendar entry, document, communication, filing/service step, list update, review, follow-up, vendor step, or close condition.
Calendar actions contain the literal desired calendar wording.
Document actions identify the exact draft or output to generate.
Case/List Update actions identify the exact system, project, list, PHS, or status record to update.
Current primary authority and controlling court/provider requirements must be verified before programming mandatory legal logic.
